channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Requiring federal workers to return to office will be hard: Comer
item.description: U.S. Rep. James Comer believes evaluating the federal workforce's efficiency is critical to eliminating federal waste.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Another Florida state representative switches from Democrat to Republican
item.description: For the second time this year, a Florida Democratic lawmaker has switched their party affiliation after winning their seat.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US to send $1.25 billion in weapons to Ukraine, pushing to get aid out before Biden leaves office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is expected to announce that it will send $1.25 billion in military assistance to Ukraine, U.S. officials said Friday, as the Biden administration pushes to get as much aid to Kyiv as possible before leaving office on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Georgia lawmakers can subpoena Fani Willis for information related to Trump case, court rules
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis can be subpoenaed by Georgia lawmakers for information related to her case against Trump, a judge ruled on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baja to lease warehouse space to house deported migrants
item.description: He also stated the warehouse location will have easier access from the ports of entry where migrants might be deported instead of having to bus them to several areas in the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: Kay Granger revelation punctuates increasing scrutiny of aging officials
item.description: The revelation that Rep. Kay Granger (R-Texas) has been living in an assisted living facility is fueling scrutiny of aging public officials, highlighting the shifting norms surrounding lawmaker health, seniority, and for how long it is appropriate to hang on to power.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Manchin objects to Biden's commutations
item.description: Outgoing Sen. Joe Manchin (I-W.Va.) is denouncing as “misguided” and “disgusting” President Biden's move to commute the sentences of two men convicted of murdering a student at Marshall University.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump changed America’s legal landscape
item.description: President-elect Trump's legal issues dominated his 2024 presidential bid, where courtroom appearances were just as common as rallies and campaign stops.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian government says it is willing to improve ties—but onus is on Trump to make first move
item.description: Retired Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg told Fox News that both Russia and Ukraine are ready for the war to end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WV Gov. Jim Justice to delay US Senate swearing-in, averting brief bedlam in Charleston
item.description: West Virginia would have had four governors in ten days if Justice were to take his Senate seat on-time, so the governor announced he will serve out his term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McHenry jokes sister sent him 'the cruelest things' about him on the internet after famous gavel slam
item.description: Rep. Patrick McHenry (R-N.C.) joked that his sister sent him “the cruelest things” people said about him on the internet after his famous gavel slam affirming the ouster of former Speaker Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif.) became a meme, the News and Observer reported.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic pols ditch Twitter after Elon Musk takeover, report shows
item.description: Democratic lawmakers have shown a sharp decline in X usage in the roughly two years since Elon Musk bought the company.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump 'border czar' says he will override sanctuary city roadblocks
item.description: "Sanctuary jurisdictions aren't going to stop what we're going to do," Tom Homan said in a NewsNation interview.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Congressional lawmakers almost got a pay bump this year. Then it crashed and burned
item.description: Lawmakers on Capitol Hill haven’t had a pay increase since 2009, representing a 31 percent decline in their salary when adjusted for inflation.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Most Americans blame insurance profits and denials alongside the killer in UHC CEO death, poll finds
item.description: Most Americans believe health insurance profits and coverage denials share responsibility for the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO — although not as much as the person who pulled the trigger.
item.dc:creator: Linley Sanders, Tom Murphy And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump has pressed for voting changes. GOP majorities in Congress will try to make that happen
item.description: Republicans plan to move quickly in their effort to overhaul the nation’s voting procedures, seeing an opportunity with control of the White House and both chambers of Congress to push through long-sought changes that include voter ID and proof-of-citizenship requirements.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump faces roadblocks to ambitious border crackdown
item.description: President-elect Trump has made ambitious promises on the border and immigration, including the biggest deportation operation ever seen. But much like in Trump’s first term in office, his immigration agenda faces significant roadblocks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Trump vows to direct DOJ to pursue death penalty in wake of Biden clemencies
item.description: President-elect Trump vowed Tuesday to direct his Department of Justice (DOJ) to pursue the death penalty in the wake of President Biden’s clemencies of nearly every federal death row inmate.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Opinion: Elon Musk wants to ‘delete’ many Americans' financial lifeline
item.description: It is especially surprising that one of the first federal agencies to come under scrutiny from the incoming administration is one that has returned billions of dollars to many of the same consumers who were counting on leaders in Washington to look out for their wallets.
item.dc:creator: Sharon McGowan, opinion contributor, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden will decide on US Steel acquisition after influential panel fails to reach consensus
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A powerful government panel on Monday failed to reach consensus on the possible national security risks of a nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase U.S. Steel, leaving the decision to President Joe Biden, who opposes the deal.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARC LEVY and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden vetoes once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal judgeships, citing 'hurried' House action
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday vetoed a once-bipartisan effort to add 66 federal district judgeships, saying “hurried action” by the House left important questions unanswered about the life-tenured positions.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside the Gaetz ethics report, a trove of new details alleging payments for sex and drug use
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House Ethics Committee's long-awaited report on Matt Gaetz documents a trove of salacious allegations, including sex with an underage girl, that tanked the Florida Republican's bid to lead the Justice Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ethics releases Matt Gaetz report: 5 takeaways
item.description: The report — which spans 42 pages and includes several more in exhibits — found “substantial evidence” that Gaetz violated House rules and state and federal laws.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Biden’s death row commutations draws chorus of mixed reactions
item.description: A chorus of supporters and critics have emerged following the commutation of nearly every federal death row inmate by President Biden, whose decision came at a time when he’s trying to frame a so-far troubled legacy.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Congress looks to ban Chinese drones widely used in US: What to know about the debate
item.description: Lawmakers in Washington are seeking to ban new sales of drones from two dominant Chinese manufacturers, arguing they could be used to spy on Americans and that the low-cost models are hurting the U.S. drone industry.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden didn't remove these 3 inmates from death row
item.description: Those who received the Monday commutations of their sentences saw them go from execution to life with no possibility of parole. However, three “hard cases” were not among the commutations.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: READ: House Ethics report on Gaetz allegations
item.description: The House Ethics Committee on Monday released its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), who has faced allegations of sexual misconduct and illicit drug use, among other violations.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Gaetz sues Ethics Committee in last-minute bid to block release of report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) filed a lawsuit Monday against the House Ethics Committee in a last-minute attempt to block the panel from releasing its report on him.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As Texas Chief Justice Nathan Hecht prepares to retire, he reflects on the Supreme Court he helped change
item.description: Over 35 years, Hecht modernized the court, increased access to justice for the poor and saw his conservative views come to dominate the bench.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: Broken tie in Minnesota House would give GOP muscle at start of 2025 session
item.description: Even with a slight majority, Republicans could flex newfound power at the start of the legislative session if DFL lawmaker’s disqualification stands.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Biden commutes sentences of 37 death row inmates to life without parole
item.description: "In good conscience, I cannot stand back and let a new administration resume executions that I halted," Biden said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: White House made 'friendliest threat' over Biden's decline: DNC committee member
item.description: According to Lindy Li, the official told her, "You're one of the President's most loyal supporters. We know you're going to stay loyal.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Biden signs bill that averts a government shutdown and brings a close to days of Washington upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden signed a bill into law Saturday that averts a government shutdown, bringing a final close to days of upheaval after Congress approved a temporary funding plan just past the deadline and refused President-elect Donald Trump’s core debt demands in the package.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What is next for Florida's insurance market as CFO Patronis steps down
item.description: He is the state's top insurance official, but Florida's Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis is stepping down in a few months to run for Congress. Governor Ron DeSantis will appoint a temporary replacement to the CFO position, as Florida residents continue to face a fragile insurance market.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
